192.reason: Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the code unconditionally reads the next character and treats it as part of "&&", without checking if the next character is actually '&'. This fails to detect the lexical error "a" (single '&' or '|' appearing individually). The commented-out error reporting line should be uncommented and the logic should be corrected to handle the case where the next character is not '&'.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the code unconditionally reads the next character and treats it as part of "&&", without checking if the next character is actually '&'. This fails to detect the lexical error "a" (single '&' or '|' appearing individually). The commented-out error reporting line should be uncommented and the logic should be corrected to handle the case where the next character is not '&'.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When encountering a single '&', the code immediately reads the next character without checking if it is also '&'. According to the problem description, a single '&' is an error (type a). The code should first check if the next character is '&'; if not, it should report error type a and treat it as a single '&' token (or handle the error appropriately). Currently, it incorrectly consumes the next character unconditionally, which can cause issues when the next character is not '&'.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: does not check if next char is '&'; should report error if single '&'
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing check if next char is '&'; also consumes an extra character incorrectly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The original code blindly consumed the next character and treated '&' followed by any character as '&&', failing to detect the error where '&' appears individually. It also failed to report the error using Printer.addErrorMsg.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The code blindly consumes the next character and treats '&' followed by any character as '&&', failing to detect the error when a single '&' appears (e.g., in "str[i] & str[i]"). It also fails to call Printer.addErrorMsg.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This assumes the next character is always '&', but if it's not (e.g., standalone '&'), it incorrectly consumes two characters and forms an invalid "&&" token instead of reporting error type 'a'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The code assumes the next character is always '&', but if it's not (e.g., standalone '&'), it should report error type 'a' instead of treating it as AND token.]

198.reason: Error: Same issue as above for single '|'. The code unconditionally reads the next character and treats it as part of "||", without checking if the next character is actually '|'. This fails to detect the lexical error "a" (single '&' or '|' appearing individually). The commented-out error reporting line should be uncommented and the logic should be corrected to handle the case where the next character is not '|'.
